---
title: "Module 1 — Foundations & Linear Regression"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: false
---

<!-- Thin module landing (repo convention: index.qmd is a landing, never a
     lecture deck). Decks live beside it; see CLAUDE.md. -->

One clinical question — *what predicts systolic blood pressure?* — introduces
supervised regression: the linear model, the mean-squared-error loss
$\mathcal{L}$, the closed-form least-squares solution via the normal equations,
and its geometry as an orthogonal projection. NHANES 2021–2022 is the dataset
throughout the term.

| Deck | Link |
|------|------|
| Lecture A — Setup, Data, and the Linear Model | [m1a-setup-and-model](m1a-setup-and-model.html) |
| Lecture B — Normal Equations & Geometry | [m1b-normal-equations](m1b-normal-equations.html) |
| Appendix — Matrix Calculus for the Normal Equations | [appendix-matrix-calculus](appendix-matrix-calculus.html) |

Companion activity: [week01 least-squares explorer](https://gchism94.github.io/info521-activities-2026/week01-least-squares/)
(embedded live in Lecture A).

[← All slide decks](../../index.html)
